description: English stopword configuration based on ground truth analysis
source: Generic stop-words library with field-specific additions
notes: English benefits from removing articles, prepositions, and conjunctions. Field-specific additions for better matching.

notes_on_exclusions.reason: These are content words that help distinguish between different works. Publisher-side corporate-suffix and form-of-publication noise was promoted to publisher_stopwords following the 2026-05-28 lower-band diagnostic; these stay scoped to publisher because removing them from title would strip distinguishing tokens from names like 'Penguin Books'. The 2026-06-14 institutional batch ('university', 'institute', 'academy', 'national', 'bureau', 'department', 'division', 'office', 'committee', 'council', 'foundation', 'museum', 'library') is publisher-only for the same reason: these words do not distinguish one academic/governmental imprint from another (every 'X University Press' shares 'university'), so on the publisher side they inflate non-matches, while on the title side they remain distinguishing content words.
